title: Bridge Essay: Thought insertion and disturbed for-me-ness (minimal selfhood) in schizophrenia.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nondual/Unity** (Phase 16) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Nondual/Unity to mechanistic neuroscience through Thought, insertion, and, disturbed, for-me-ness.

**Thought insertion and disturbed for-me-ness (minimal selfhood) in schizophrenia.** — Conscious Cogn. PMID: 31276908. DOI: 10.1016/j.concog.2019.102770. In contemporary consciousness research, we have defended aposition of experiential minimalism, arguing that for-me-ness (or minimal selfhood) is anecessary, universal feature of phenomenal consciousness. The concept of for-me-ness refers to the fact that experiences are given first-personally to the subject of experience. To challenge the universality of for-me-ness, several authors have referred to the case of thought insertion as aclear counter example. In this study, we address and refute
